comparison_scope.scope_summary: Conceptual diff between xhigh and high variants of the ARC-series map and its empirical hardening pass.

prompt_fit.prompt_summary: Create a consistent multi-layered but unitary ARC-series map and identify the optimal missing arcs with a proof sketch.
prompt_fit.summary: Both answer the prompt. The xhigh variant fits the recursive self-improvement policy spine more completely; the high variant fits the immediate repo-harness and workflow-seam reading more concretely.
strengths: both treat the series as a unitary layered harness
both make V68 cartography the first next step
both avoid treating support prose as lock authority
weaknesses: xhigh_v0 underweights semantic ingress, commit/release authority, and adversarial review as immediate operational seams
high_v0 underweights explicit ratification, rollback, outcome review, operator projection, and machine-checkable seed structure

prompt_fit.prompt_summary: Design and run an epistemic loop using existing repo reasoning tools, then harden the original map with empirical successes and failures.
prompt_fit.summary: Both run a real tool-mediated hardening pass. The xhigh variant formalizes tool applicability and schema seams better; the high variant records broader current-horizon command evidence better.
strengths: both use repo-native scripts and tests rather than only prose
both identify V45-C dependency-register limits as evidence for V68
both keep V68 as the next concrete family
weaknesses: xhigh_v1 records a namespace-mismatched ARC=67 check rather than the current V67-C implementation arc check
high_v1 lacks a machine-readable diff or mapping seed and keeps the six-family set without separately preserving ratification/outcome/operator-projection cuts

shared: the ARC series is a layered harness rather than a flat backlog
V68 cartography is missing and should come next
V45-C is useful precedent but not whole-series cartography
V67 is a meaningful current closure horizon
xhigh_emphasis: ratification, outcome review, operator projection, and execution widening as distinct objects
high_emphasis: whole repo family map including V31 and early vNext baseline
V43 as an unclosed general contest branch
tensions: xhigh decomposes by recursive governance cuts; high decomposes by near-term workflow authority shortcuts
xhigh treats V43 as deferred; high treats V43 as part of the minimal missing set
odeu_axes.recommended_synthesis: Represent recursive governance cuts and operational shortcut blockers as two layers in the next map rather than forcing a six-versus-eight family decision too early.

shared: tool results constrain but do not globally prove conceptual completeness
test selection is local and approximate
closeout lints are strong within their declared surface
the V45-C live derivation failure is important negative evidence
xhigh_emphasis: constitutional coherence checkers as advisory and seed-bound
high_emphasis: current V67-C closeout lint using arc 187
stop-gate deterministic environment failure and rerun
tensions: xhigh is stronger on epistemic typing of evidence; high is stronger on concrete current-horizon command breadth
xhigh embeds JSON results; high provides more readable command transcripts
odeu_axes.recommended_synthesis: Use xhigh's tool-applicability schema and high's broader current-horizon command set in a v2 empirical appendix.

shared: support docs do not authorize runtime or lock-level changes
planning text cannot become release truth without evidence
local writes and worker outputs do not mint authority by themselves
tool success cannot widen scope beyond the tool's declared contract
xhigh_emphasis: decision projection must not mint authority
execution widening must wait for recursive gates
high_emphasis: raw utterance is not task law
local mutation is not commit or release authority
multi-agent output is not native truth without settlement
host text is not self-authorizing contest law
tensions: xhigh makes ratification explicit but leaves commit/release authority less foregrounded
high makes commit/release explicit but folds ratification and outcome review into a broader experiment ledger
odeu_axes.recommended_synthesis: The next map should make commit/release authority an explicit slice or family while retaining separate ratification, rollback, and outcome-review cuts.

shared: both variants are useful inputs for V68 planning
both improve over ad hoc prose by naming seams and boundaries
both converge on a descriptive non-authorizing next step
high_emphasis: better evidence for current V67-C closeout and ergonomic tests
tensions: xhigh is more complete but can feel abstract
high is more actionable but can compress distinct recursive-policy gates
odeu_axes.recommended_synthesis: Use high as the pragmatic V68 starter-plan skeleton and xhigh as the recursive governance correctness envelope.

shared_claims.summary: All four docs treat themselves as support-layer maps rather than lock or implementation authority.
Both model variants read the ARC sequence as one layered philosophy-engineering harness.
Both variants converge on V68 as the next concrete family for current arc-series cartography.
Both empirical passes treat V45-C as useful precedent but insufficient for current whole-series cartography.
Both empirical passes reject clean tool exits as global conceptual proof.

divergences.xhigh_claim: The minimal recursive self-improvement seam set is V68 through V75: cartography, candidate intake, classification, ratification, contained integration, outcome review, operator projection, and execution widening.
divergences.high_claim: The minimal missing set is V68, V69, V70, V71, V72, and V43: cartography, semantic ingress, commit/release authority, experiment ledger, adversarial review, and general contest participation.
divergences.consequence: The two variants optimize for different notions of minimality: policy-loop completeness versus immediate authority shortcuts.
divergences.synthesis_judgment: Do not simply union or choose one. First classify high's operational seams as slice obligations or separate families inside the xhigh recursive lifecycle.

divergences.xhigh_claim: V69 is recursive candidate advancement intake and O/E/D/U mapping.
divergences.high_claim: V69 is semantic ingress / operator binding / turn compiler.
divergences.consequence: xhigh protects candidate provenance; high protects the live turn boundary where user utterance could become task law.
divergences.synthesis_judgment: V69 should likely combine both: recursive candidate intake plus closed-world operator ingress and O/E/D/U binding.

divergences.xhigh_claim: Contained integration, rollback, and later dispatch widening cover implementation movement but do not foreground git commit, PR, merge, or release authority.
divergences.high_claim: Commit, merge, and release authority is a separate missing family because V64/V65 do not promote writes into released truth.
divergences.consequence: The high variant exposes a concrete authority gap that xhigh under-specifies.
divergences.synthesis_judgment: Promote commit/release authority into the next synthesis, either as a V72 sublane or as a distinct family if it cannot be owned by contained integration.

divergences.xhigh_claim: Ratification, integration/rollback, outcome review, and operator projection are separate family-level cuts.
divergences.high_claim: Experiment ledger and adversarial settlement carry much of the adoption logic.
divergences.consequence: The high variant is more compact but risks collapsing decision, execution, review, and projection boundaries.
divergences.synthesis_judgment: Keep these cuts explicit even if later planning chooses to pack some as slices.

divergences.xhigh_claim: V43 is a meaningful connected branch but deferred outside the minimal recursive self-improvement seam set.
divergences.high_claim: V43 is part of the minimal missing set because V42 is ARC-specific and does not generalize host-world law.
divergences.consequence: The variants disagree about whether external-world generalization is prerequisite or adjacent.
divergences.synthesis_judgment: Treat V43 as connected-but-conditional unless the next planning horizon selects external contest/task intake as the immediate pressure.

divergences.xhigh_claim: The map includes a machine-checkable seed.
divergences.high_claim: The map is human-readable and structured but has no embedded JSON seed.
divergences.consequence: xhigh is easier to validate and carry into schema work; high is easier to read as prose.
divergences.synthesis_judgment: Future versions should keep a machine-readable seed while borrowing high's clearer dependency cone and negative laws.

divergences.xhigh_claim: ARC=67 closeout check passed but was namespace-limited and did not validate family V67.
divergences.high_claim: Arc bundle lint was run against arc 187 for the current V67-C horizon and passed.
divergences.consequence: xhigh correctly identified a namespace hazard; high selected the more relevant current implementation arc.
divergences.synthesis_judgment: V2 should include both: namespace hazard as a finding and arc 187 as the correct current closeout evidence.

divergences.xhigh_claim: Stress run foregrounded recursive coordinates, semantic-source zero blocks, constitutional advisory posture, and tool-result applicability.
divergences.high_claim: Stress run foregrounded stop-gate environment fail-closed behavior, V45-A fixture equality, V45-C derivation failure, and V67 ergonomic test coverage.
divergences.consequence: Each variant sees evidence the other mostly lacks.
divergences.synthesis_judgment: The empirical record should be merged, with every command classified by real output surface and authority effect.

divergences.xhigh_claim: V1 adds schema seams and an embedded JSON seed with tool-result records.
divergences.high_claim: V1 proposes a future arc_series_cartography@1 schema but does not emit a current report schema.
divergences.consequence: xhigh better satisfies the schema/hardening aspect of the prompt.
divergences.synthesis_judgment: Keep xhigh's immediate schema/report posture and import high's proposed cartography checks.

divergences.xhigh_claim: The hardened missing set remains eight families with sharper V68/V70/V73 obligations.
divergences.high_claim: The next concrete family should remain V68 with a starter arc_series_cartography@1 experiment.
divergences.consequence: xhigh keeps the full policy horizon visible; high gives the clearer next engineering move.
divergences.synthesis_judgment: Use high's V68 starter experiment as the next action, governed by xhigh's broader recursive lifecycle map.

pros: explicit O/E/D/U candidate mapping, ratification, rollback, outcome review, operator projection, and execution widening
cons: less concrete about semantic ingress from live user turns
less concrete about git commit, PR, merge, and release authority
treats V43 as outside the minimal set without deeply arguing external-world intake
variant_assessments.best_use: Use as the correctness envelope for recursive policy completeness.

pros: clearer whole-series family map including V31 and early vNext baseline
stronger immediate operational seams: semantic ingress, commit/release, experiment ledger, adversarial review
good negative laws and dependency cone
cons: six-family minimality compresses distinct ratification, rollback, outcome review, and operator projection cuts
V43 inclusion is plausible but not clearly necessary for recursive self-improvement absent external-world pressure
variant_assessments.best_use: Use as the pragmatic workflow-seam and dependency-cone source for a v2 synthesis.

pros: best formal tool-applicability and schema posture
captures coordinate absence, constitutional advisory limits, semantic-source zero-block evidence, and API-shape correction
cons: initial ARC=67 closeout run was a useful namespace lesson but not the current V67-C check
less empirical coverage of stop-gate metrics, V45-A fixture equality, and V67 ergonomic tests
variant_assessments.best_use: Use as the empirical schema and authority-effect layer for conceptual diff and future V68 planning.

pros: uses current V67-C arc 187 closeout evidence
adds stop-gate deterministic environment failure, V45-A direct fixture comparison, and V67 test evidence
cons: keeps the six-family set without preserving separate ratification/outcome/operator-projection cuts
less explicit about recursive-coordinate and constitutional-advisory surfaces
variant_assessments.best_use: Use as the empirical command appendix and V68 starter-plan source.

synthesis_recommendations.summary: Make V68 the next concrete family, but define it as cartography plus namespace, evidence, branch posture, tool applicability, and coordinate posture.
synthesis_recommendations.reason: Both variants converge on V68, and their differences are complementary.

synthesis_recommendations.summary: Merge recursive candidate intake with semantic ingress/operator binding in the next synthesis.
synthesis_recommendations.reason: Candidate provenance and live operator binding are two halves of the same admission boundary.

synthesis_recommendations.summary: Add explicit commit, PR, merge, and release authority handling to the recursive lifecycle map.
synthesis_recommendations.target_surface: V72 slice or separate family decision
synthesis_recommendations.reason: The high variant exposes a concrete authority seam not explicit enough in xhigh.

synthesis_recommendations.summary: Keep adversarial/multi-agent review as an explicit adoption-relevant phase, but decide later whether it is its own family or a V70/V71/V72 slice.
imports_from_high: no child-agent output as native truth
synthesis_recommendations.reason: Review settlement affects recursive adoption but may not require an independent family if the lifecycle families own it cleanly.

synthesis_recommendations.summary: Track V43 as connected-but-conditional rather than silently dropping or automatically including it.
imports_from_high: host-world law and general contest participation argument
synthesis_recommendations.reason: V43 is real and unreleased, but its minimality depends on whether external-world intake is selected as immediate pressure.

open_questions: Should commit/merge/release authority be a separate family, or can V72 own it without collapsing rollback and release-truth boundaries?
Should multi-agent adversarial review be family-level, or a required evidence phase inside V70/V71?
Does V69 become too broad if it owns both recursive candidate intake and live operator turn compilation?
At what horizon does V43 become prerequisite rather than connected deferred branch?
